content.text: You can install pygame-dom using pip in terminal:
Pygame-dom provides 3 important classes: DOM, UIPage and UIEvent:

code: from pygame_dom import DOM, UIPage, UIEvent
import pygame # You have to also import pygame for other stuff like creating the window or getting events

content.text: To create your first UIPage from a HTML file you can use:

code: page: UIPage = DOM.create_ui_page("page.html") # "page.html" - path to HTML file

content.text: Pages can be styled using a CSS file:

code: page.style("page.css") # "page.css" - path to CSS file

content.text: Pages can be rendered inside of the pygame's game loop and require screen (pygame.Surface) and events (list[pygame.event.Event]). Example below shows how to get events from pygame and how to render the page.

code: events: list[pygame.event.Event] = pygame.event.get() # Get list of events inside of the pygame's gameloop
page.render(screen, events) # Render page in the pygame's gameloop

content.text: <span style='color: red'>It is important to use pygame.event.get() only once in the pygame's gameloop. Pygame's pygame.event.get() returns the events and it also removes them from the occured events list.<span>
